rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BARIBEAU VS. HARRY C. THOMAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Deeming Matters In Defendant'S Requests For Admission As Admitted By Plaintiff, Or, Alternatively, An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To The Requests For Admissions Without Objections
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 1, Motion 3 DEFENDANT BROWN & CO. REAL ESTATE, INC.'s Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Deeming Matters In Defendant'S Requests For Admission As Admitted By Plaintiff, Or, Alternatively, An Order Compelling Plaintiff To Respond To The Requests For Admissions Without Objections Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to discuss (1) good faith efforts to resolve the issue of electronic service with regard to the three related motions set for hearing on June 25, 2018; (2) defense counsel's reliance upon CRC Rule 8.78 during meet and confer efforts; (3) relevant law governing agreement to accept electronic service, including California Rule of Court 2.251 and CCP section 1010.6; (4) any case law precedent related to plaintiff's argument that local rules and California Rules of Court do not apply to electronic service of discovery documents; and (5) sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = MTD

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BARIBEAU VS. HARRY C. THOMAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Joseph Baribeau'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Inspection Demand, Set One
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 1, Motion 1 Plaintiff Joseph Baribeau'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Inspection Demand, Set One Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to address request for sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendants Laura Rogers and Brown & Company Real Estate, Inc.'s Responses to Plaintiff's Inspection Demand, Set One is denied. Plaintiff does not dispute he received defendants' responses and signed verifications via electronic mail on April 16, 2018, but argues he did not agree to accept electronic service and therefore the responses and verifications are invalid and defendants have waived objections. This argument is not persuasive in light of the submitted March 2018 email wherein defense counsel states that responses will be sent "via email" and plaintiff's counsel states "no problem." As to the issue of the validity of signed verifications provided by email, plaintiff's reliance on Thomas v. Makita, U.S.A., Inc. (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 989 is misplaced. The Makita court did not address the issue of verifications provided by email, but rather the situation where one party's attorney lost the verification and therefore did not provide it to the opposing party. (Id. at 992-993.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = MTD

rulings.Case Title: AZURE SKY DUFAUX ET AL VS. CHRISTOPHER HANSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Christopher Hanson And Hanson Law Firm, P.C.S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 3, Defendants Christopher Hanson And Hanson Law Firm, P.C.S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Defendants Christopher Hanson and Hanson Law Firm, P.C.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued per CCP 437c(h) to January 30, 2019 to permit the Trustee to conduct discovery and file supplemental opposition papers. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed no later than January 11, 2019 and supplemental reply papers must be filed no later than January 23, 2019. The bankruptcy issues stalled the discovery process and the Trustee has had little opportunity to conduct discovery. There is no legal prejudice to the Hanson defendants in continuing the hearing. If the Hanson parties prefer, they can withdraw this motion and file a new summary motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Trustee is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Case Title: NRIPENDRA DHILLON VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendants The Regents of the University of California and Alan Busbaum's motion for summary judgment on the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Nripendra Dhillon is denied as to the first cause of action for retaliation which is only alleged against the Regents and is granted as to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress which is only alleged against Dr. Basbaum. Liberally construing the evidence presented by Dr. Dhillon, that evidence creates a triable dispute whether Dr. Dhillon's protected conduct of complaining that he was discriminated against was a substantial factor in the reduction of Dr. Dhillon's appointment percentage, salary and benefits for the 2016-17 academic year. The undisputed facts establish that Dr. Busbaum did not commit any extreme or outrageous conduct and Dr. Dhillon did suffer severe emotional distress. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Regents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC VS. TALESUN SOLAR USA LTD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Talesun Solar Usa Ltd. And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 2 PLAINTIFF MARTIFER-SILVERADO FUND I, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable Of Defendant And Cross-Complainant Talesun Solar Usa Ltd. And Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Plaintiff should be prepared to address the statement in their Reply: "¿Talesun argues that it cannot be compelled to produce Chinese residents for deposition in this country (knowing full well that China entirely forbids attorneys from taking depositions within its borders for use in American courts)." Defendant should be prepared to address (1) why they contend Toyota Motor Corp. v. Superior Court (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1107 should apply to the deposition of a corporation's Person Most Qualified to Testify and (2) whether Talesun Solar will be present to testify at trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = MTD

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL MANUEL GARCIA VS. ESTEVEZ MARGARITO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT ESTEVEZ MARGARITO's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendant Estevez Margarito's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Ruth Garcia and Claudia Perez is denied. Liberally construed, the allegations of the complaint allege that Mr. Estevez chose to drive a vehicle knowing that he was intoxicated and could not drive safely. This is sufficient to show, for pleading purposes, despicable conduct carried on with a willful and conscious disregard for the safety of others. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THOMAS JOSEPH SASSANI VS. KLP ENTERPRISES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANT BRIAN BARTH's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Brian Barth's demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the twenty-second cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Thomas Sassani is overruled as to both causes of action. Mr. Sassani adequately alleges ultimate facts as to each element of a derivative breach of fiduciary claim on behalf of Ekoventure, Inc. A breach of fiduciary claim is a sufficient predicate to allege a UCL claim based on the unlawful prong of that statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CARL A. WESCOTT VS. JOHN FIELDS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 1 PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT'S Motion For A Protective Order Line 3 CGC-17-558198 CARL A. WESCOTT VS. JOHN FIELDS ET AL Motion For A Protective Order Moving Party: CARL WESCOTT Filed: May 10, 2018 Tentative Ruling: Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion For Protective Order is denied. Plaintiff failed to meet his burden to show good cause for the order sought. (Fairmont Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (2000) Cal. 4th 245, 255.) Further, the preponderance of the evidence indicates plaintiff raised questions of mail service to negotiate other matters. Sanctions are awarded against plaintiff in the amount of $450. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = MTD

rulings.Case Title: GILLIAN ASHDOWN VS. ALEXANDER PETERSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 5 PLAINTIFF GILLIAN ASHDOWN'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Sanctions are awarded against defendant in the amount of $500. The discovery at issue in this motion was not burdensome - simply contact information for two witnesses identified in defendant's discovery responses. Plaintiff offered defendant additional time to provide this information if defendant would agree to a corresponding extension of time for a motion to compel. Defendant refused, and did not provide his further response to the interrogatories at issue until two weeks after plaintiff's motion was filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mtderish@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, JUNE 25, 2018, LINE 6 DEFENDANTS DANIEL BECK and BECK LAW, P.C.'S Motion To Compel And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Melinda Derish, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion of defendants Daniel B. Beck and Beck Law, P.C. (collectively "Beck") to compel and for sanctions is unopposed and granted. Plaintiff Benjamin Cannon must produce Code-compliant verified responses to the interrogatories and requests for production no later than July 16, 2018. The genuineness of any documents and the truth of any matters specified in Beck's requests for admission are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against plaintiff in the amount of $444 - a reduction of 3 hours at the hourly rate stated in defense counsel's declaration - to reflect the fact that defendant's request for sanctions included 3 hours for reviewing and replying to an opposition. rulings.Case Title: GANNAWAY ENTERRAINMENT, INC. ET AL VS. FRANKLY, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT SKP AMERICA, LLCS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Hearing required. At the hearing the Court will inquire why defendants did not file a fully briefed motion to compel arbitration concurrently with their demurrers. The Court is inclined not to rule on the demurrers until after defendants' request to compel arbitration are appropriately briefed and decided or defendants state that they do not seek an order compelling arbitration. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: GANNAWAY ENTERRAINMENT, INC. ET AL VS. FRANKLY, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT LOUIS SCHWARTZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Defendant Louis Schwartz's motion to quash service of summons for lack of personal jurisdiction is denied. Plaintiffs satisfied their burden to show that this court has personal jurisdiction over Mr. Schwartz per specific jurisdiction principles. Mr. Schwartz purposefully availed himself of the benefits of California and his California contacts relate to plaintiffs' fraud claims. Plaintiffs presented evidence that Mr. Schwartz traveled to California for a meeting with Mr. Chung to discuss Frankly, Inc.'s acquisition of WorldNow Media, forwarded Mr. Chung's emails to plaintiffs, and sent WorldNow employees to California to facilitate the acquisition. Mr. Schwartz also received benefits from and engaged in significant activities in California in his capacity as CEO of Frankly Media, which, until recently, was headquartered in San Francisco. Mr. Schwartz has not shown that requiring him to defend this action in California is unreasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT ARTURO DEVESA'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Defendant Arturo Devesa's motion to quash service of summons is denied. Substituted service per CCP 415.20(b) was properly made on Mr. Devesa. Plaintiffs acted with reasonable diligence in trying to personally serve Mr. Devesa after finding out that Mr. Devesa no longer lived at his last known address by attempting service two times at the address for MedWhat.com Inc. listed on its website, a company that Mr. Davesa served as CEO, and three times at the address for Mr. Devesa's other company, Linda HealthCare Corporation Corp, listed on the company's website. Plaintiffs' subsequent substituted service at the address listed on MedWhat's website substantially complied with the statutory requirements. The office manager of the company managing the shared office space who accepted service apparently knew Mr. Devesa and her statements showed that it was more likely than not that the served papers would be delivered to Mr. Devesa. Although Mr. Devesa states that this address is a PO Box and that "ended" on May 1, 2018, the substituted service was effected on April 30, 2018 and substituted service can be effected at a private PO box. (Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 546). Mr. Devesa has had actual notice of the case since May 14, 2018 when he retained counsel to represent him. Mr. Devesa must respond to the complaint no later than July 13, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IKH HOLDINGS LTD., A BVI LIMITED COMPANY VS. ARTTWO50, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Note Purchase Agreement, Convertible Promissory Note, Amendment To Convertible Promissory Note; Declaration Of Matthew S. Kenefick
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 25, 2018, Line 12, PLAINTIFF IKH HOLDINGS LTD., A BVI LIMITED COMPANY'S Motion To Seal Note Purchase Agreement, Convertible Promissory Note, Amendment To Convertible Promissory Note IKH Holdings LTD's motion to seal note purchase agreement, convertible promissory note, and amendment to convertible promissory note is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown per CRC 2.550. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =302/hk
